name: WebSocket Heartbeat
summary: Heartbeat message frame used to keep the WebSocket connection alive. The client sends a frame with t="h" every 30 seconds and the server replies with a heartbeat acknowledgment frame (t="hk") that also carries a timestamp in seconds.

json_fields.name: t
json_fields.description: Task type for the WebSocket request frame. 'h' represents the Heartbeat task. Sent by the client to keep the connection alive.

response_fields.name: t
response_fields.description: Task type of the response frame. 'hk' represents Heartbeat acknowledgment.

response_fields.name: hk
response_fields.description: Timestamp in seconds (heartbeat acknowledgment timestamp). [Source prints the typo 'timmstamps in seconds'.]

notes: This is a WebSocket message frame, not a REST endpoint: the connection is over wss:// (not https://), and the documented "curl" example is illustrative of the JSON frame payload rather than a literal runnable HTTP call. The heartbeat frame {"t":"h"} must be sent every 30 seconds to keep the connection alive; the server acknowledges with a frame whose t="hk". The RESPONSE table has two rows: t (Possible value 'hk') and hk (a timestamp in seconds). On the page the 'timmstamps in seconds' text sits in the Description column of the hk row, and that row's Possible-value cell is EMPTY (the draft incorrectly placed the timestamp text as the hk field's possible_value). The source contains typos: 'actknowledgment' (acknowledgment) and 'timmstamps' (timestamp). No query parameters (no jData/jKey) - heartbeat is a pure WebSocket frame. The page does NOT print any verbatim success-response or failure-response JSON block for the heartbeat acknowledgment, so sample_success_response and sample_failure_response are left empty rather than reconstructed. Page note: page 83 opens with the tail of the preceding 'Unsubscribe Position update' section (t=upk, excluded) and ends by starting the next 'USER DETAILS' section (also excluded).
issues: No verbatim success or failure JSON response block is printed in the source for the heartbeat acknowledgment; the success-response object is intentionally left empty (the prior draft reconstructed {"t":"hk"}, which is not verbatim). The RESPONSE 'hk' field's possible-value cell is empty in the image - the 'timmstamps in seconds' text belongs to the Description column, not the Possible-value column. Source typos: 'timmstamps', 'actknowledgment'. The curl example uses wss:// with --data, which is not literal curl-runnable syntax but conveys the frame payload.
